file_insights.code_quality: High - comprehensive docstrings, clear enum definitions, consistent formatting
file_insights.django_patterns: Follows Django TextChoices pattern correctly with tuple pairs (value, display_name)

patterns_discovered.description: All enums properly extend models.TextChoices with tuple pairs for both storage value and human-readable display
Enums grouped by business domain (status enums for lifecycle management, action enums for audit trail, scope enums for RBAC)
AuditAction enum organized into logical groups (CRUD, Authentication, Authorization, Account, Data Operations, Subscription, System)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Improved maintainability and clarity of audit operations

patterns_discovered.description: Module-level docstring with usage example, class-level docstrings explaining purpose and valid values

gotchas_discovered.detail: Unlike other enums which use UPPERCASE (e.g., 'ACTIVE'), PermissionAction uses lowercase (e.g., 'view', 'list'). This is intentional for resource.action pattern convention.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Minimal - intentional design choice for permission naming pattern

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Unused SessionStatus and PermissionAction in original requirements
gotchas_discovered.detail: Original subtask specified 5 enums (OrganizationStatus, UserStatus, BranchStatus, RoleScope, AuditAction) but implementation includes 2 additional enums (SessionStatus, PermissionAction)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Scope creep - adds 2 enums beyond requirements
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Additional enums are well-documented and follow established patterns, likely anticipating future model needs

gotchas_discovered.description: AuditAction has 22 enum values, significantly more than other status enums (typically 3). Risk of audit log explosion.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Database bloat if all actions are logged
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Should be paired with selective audit logging strategy at model level

approach_outcome.implementation_quality: High - follows Django best practices, comprehensive documentation, clear enum grouping

recommendations.recommendation: Consider splitting AuditAction into multiple related enums if audit categories expand further (e.g., AuthAuditAction, DataAuditAction)
recommendations.rationale: Current implementation is manageable at 22 values but could become unwieldy with future additions

recommendations.recommendation: Add unit tests to verify enum values are used consistently across models
recommendations.rationale: Enum choice values are critical for database integrity and migrations

recommendations.recommendation: Create a choices reference guide showing which enums are used in which models
recommendations.rationale: Developers should easily understand where each enum is applied

recommendations.recommendation: Document the intentional use of lowercase in PermissionAction versus UPPERCASE in other enums
recommendations.rationale: Prevents confusion during future maintenance or new enum creation

recommendations.recommendation: Define audit logging strategy for which AuditAction values should trigger logging (all vs. selective)
recommendations.rationale: Prevents potential performance issues from excessive logging of READ operations
